title: Causal Modelling

prerequisites.text: Acceptance into Master's programs in Statistics & Data Science, Data Science or Biostatistics, and/or statistical background deemed sufficient by the Head of Department.

outline: This course introduces students to the concept of causality, causal diagrams and causal modelling. Topics covered include Counterfactual Theory, Directed Acyclical Graphs, Propensity Scores, Inverse Probability Weighting, Marginal Structural Models, G-estimation, Path Analysis, Confirmatory Factor Analysis, Structural Equation Modelling (SEM), Multiple Group SEM, MIMIC Models, Multilevel SEM, and Latent Growth Curve SEM. The course covers both theory and application using computer software such as R, STATA and LISREL.
assessment: Assignments 40%. Written exam 60%. Sub-minimum of 40% in each of assignment and examination component.
